cards.title: North Pole (Negative Pole) — Properties
cards._legacyHtml:
Polarity: Negative / North-seeking pole.
Properties: Sedating, cooling, anti-inflammatory, inhibitory, bacteriostatic, analgesic.
Indications: Inflammation, infections, pain, hypertension, insomnia, anxiety, tumours (adjunct), fever, acute conditions.
Mechanism: Decreases cellular activity, reduces oedema, inhibits bacterial growth, slows nerve conduction velocity.
Application: Place north pole directly over affected area. 20–45 min daily.
Mnemonic: North = No inflammation.

cards.title: South Pole (Positive Pole) — Properties
cards._legacyHtml:
Polarity: Positive / South-seeking pole.
Properties: Stimulating, warming, activating, anabolic, promotes growth and metabolism.
Indications: Paralysis, muscle weakness, hypothyroidism, chronic fatigue, fracture healing, wound healing (chronic), depression, malnutrition.
Mechanism: Increases cellular activity, promotes blood supply, stimulates nerve conduction, enhances tissue regeneration.
Application: Place south pole over affected area. 20–30 min. Caution: Do NOT use in infections, tumours, or fever.

cards.title: Static Magnets — Application Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Types: Ceramic, neodymium (rare earth), flexible magnetic belts, magnetic insoles, magnetic jewellery.
Strength: Therapeutic range 300–3000 Gauss (local); up to 10,000 Gauss for deeper tissue.
Application Rules:
  • Acute: North pole first
  • Chronic/weakness: South pole
  • Bilateral: North on affected side, South on opposite for balance
Duration: 20–60 min/session, 1–2× daily. Course: 3–4 weeks minimum.
Bipolar Magnets: Use when unclear — provides balanced effect.

cards.title: PEMF Therapy (Pulsed Electromagnetic Field)
cards._legacyHtml:
Mechanism: Low-frequency pulsed magnetic fields (1–100 Hz) stimulate cell membrane ion channels, enhance ATP production, modulate inflammation, promote bone and tissue repair.
Evidence: Grade A for non-union fractures; Grade B for OA pain, osteoporosis.
Indications: Non-union fractures, osteoporosis, OA, LBP, depression (rTMS), wound healing, peripheral neuropathy.
Parameters: 1–50 Hz, 1–100 Gauss. Session: 20–30 min. Course: 15–20 sessions.
Devices: PEMF mat, localised applicators, helmet (for brain targets).

cards.title: Magnetic Water
cards._legacyHtml:
Preparation: Place two 1000–3000 Gauss magnets on opposite sides of a glass of water (north pole touching glass). Leave for 12–24 hours.
North pole water: Anti-inflammatory, astringent. Use for UTI, kidney stones, hypertension, infections. Dose: 60–90 mL 3× daily.
South pole water: Stimulant, tonic. Use for weakness, paralysis, hypothyroidism. Dose: 60 mL 2× daily.
Storage: Use within 24–48 hours. Keep away from other magnets after preparation.

cards.title: Contraindications — Magnet Therapy
cards._legacyHtml:
Absolute Contraindications:
  • Cardiac pacemaker / implanted electronic devices
  • Pregnancy (avoid over abdomen/lumbar)
  • Bleeding disorders / anticoagulant therapy
  • Active cancer (do not use south pole over tumour)
  • Metallic implants (some types — consult manufacturer)
Relative Contraindications:
  • Acute infections (south pole)
  • Children under 5 years (reduce strength/duration)
  • Immediately post-surgery (first 48 hours)
Note: Remove watches, credit cards, and electronics before treatment.

cards.title: Safety Guidelines & Application Tips
cards._legacyHtml:
Identification of Poles: Use a compass — north pole of magnet repels compass north needle. Label magnets clearly.
Storage: Store magnets in pairs (north-to-south) in wooden or plastic box. Keep away from electronics.
Handling: Neodymium magnets >500 Gauss can cause pinching injuries — handle carefully.
Dose Principle: Start low (300–500 G, 20 min) and titrate up based on response. Too much south pole can aggravate if infection is present.
Documentation: Record pole used, Gauss strength, area, duration, and patient response each session.
